tossups.question: In one scene in this musical, a character acts out three characters who discuss Michelle’s inability to find a husband in a parody called “Show Me How to Pray.” In another scene in this musical, a preacher gives a sermon on how “the only thing worse than dying of AIDS would be living with it” after recalling his cousin Darnell’s refusal of HIV medication. The mother of this musical’s protagonist calls him on his birthday to “remind [him] / periodically” that she loves him before telling him that “hell is real.” The main character of this musical notes “on days his (*) blackness feels like another hurdle” in a song that describes how “white girls can do anything, can’t they / black boys must always obey their mothers.” The main character of this musical is asked “how many minutes ‘til the end of intermission,” while debating how he should write a new “big, black, queer-ass, American, Broadway show.” For 10 points, what 2022 Tony Award winner for Best Musical depicts writer Michael L. Jackson’s attempt at writing the musical, creating the title cyclical concept?
tossups.question_sanitized: In one scene in this musical, a character acts out three characters who discuss Michelle's inability to find a husband in a parody called "Show Me How to Pray." In another scene in this musical, a preacher gives a sermon on how "the only thing worse than dying of AIDS would be living with it" after recalling his cousin Darnell's refusal of HIV medication. The mother of this musical's protagonist calls him on his birthday to "remind [him] / periodically" that she loves him before telling him that "hell is real." The main character of this musical notes "on days his (*) blackness feels like another hurdle" in a song that describes how "white girls can do anything, can't they / black boys must always obey their mothers." The main character of this musical is asked "how many minutes 'til the end of intermission," while debating how he should write a new "big, black, queer-ass, American, Broadway show." For 10 points, what 2022 Tony Award winner for Best Musical depicts writer Michael L. Jackson's attempt at writing the musical, creating the title cyclical concept?

tossups.question: Description acceptable. One usage of this technique is represented by the leftmost gift given to Jesus in the mosaic of the southwestern entrance of the Hagia Sophia. André Gide (“jeed”) coined a French term to describe this technique exemplified by a Giotto (“JO-toh”) altarpiece in which Cardinal Stefaneschi holds up an object. This technique is used in a painting of a man staring at a harbor called Print Gallery. Jan Musset’s design of a nurse on a cocoa box using this technique is the origin of the (*) Droste effect. A lithograph of two hands drawn with this technique sometimes called mises en abyme is cited in a Douglas Hofstadter book that compares Kurt Gödel, J.S. Bach, and M.C. Escher. For 10 points, name this technique eventually limited by resolution in which an artwork refers back to itself.
tossups.answer: recursion [accept self-reference; accept strange loop; accept Droste effect until read; accept mises en abyme until read; accept descriptions of artwork containing itself or artwork creating itself; accept descriptions of artwork looping or nesting within itself; prompt on paradoxes; reject “fractals” or “infinite loops”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: recursion [accept self-reference; accept strange loop; accept Droste effect until read; accept mises en abyme until read; accept descriptions of artwork containing itself or artwork creating itself; accept descriptions of artwork looping or nesting within itself; prompt on paradoxes; reject "fractals" or "infinite loops"]
